California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmission
February 27, 
■99■
John R. I,lccarthy, P . E.
Director of Pubfic Works
city of El Paso de Robles
P. O. Box 307
Paso Robles , CA 93447-0307
Rer Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-91-10 0
Dear Mr. Mccarthy:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing your responsibi l ities as Director of Public works and cj-ty
Engineer for the cj.ty of EI Paso de Robles under the confl-ict-of-
inferest provisj-ons ot ttre Political Reform Act (the nasgrr1.1
OUESTI ON
As Director of Public works and city Engineer for the city of
El Paso de Robles may you participate in governmental decisions
which rnay affect your former enployer?
CONCLUSION
According to your facts, it does not appear that your prior
employment with uorth Coast Engineering, Inc. will result in a
conflict of interest under the PoIitica1 Reform Act.
FACTS
on January 2, :-991, you were appointed to the position of
public Works Dlrector and City Engineer for the City of El Paso de
RobLes. As part of your offiaial-duties 
you supervise the city's
""qi""..i"g 
iirri.ionl 
your job also entails the authority to sign

i[: t°
 Title 2′
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804Ю807 ● (916)322‐ 5660


Page 2
engineering plans for the city, make recommendations regarding
development proposals, and provide project inspection services.
Accordinq to the inforrnation provided by you,2 for five
years, prior to your appointment, you lirere employed by North coast
Engineering, Inc. ('tNorth Coastr') which provides civil engineering
services to private cl-ients and to the city. 
You received income
from North coast as an employee of North Coast, all of which was
rece j-ved or accrued prior to the tirne you becarne a public
official. 
You possess no ownership interest in North coast and do
not intend to return to North coast as an employee in the future.
North coast continues to be actj.ve before the city and
regularl-y subrnits plans to the city engineering division for
approval. You have become concerned that your prior employrnent
with North Coast will create a conflict of interest with respect
to decisions that nay directJ-y and indirectly affect North Coast.
ANALYSIS
section 87100 prohibits any public officia] 
from rnaking,
participating in rnaking, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official 
has a financial interest. 
A 'rpublic official" 
is defined
in Regulation 18700 (copy enclosed) to include every natural
person who is a menber, officer, enployee, or consultant of a
state or Ioca1 government agency. (Section A2O4A. ) This
definition woul,d include your position as director of publ j,c works
and city engineer.
Section 87103 specifies that an officiat 
has a financial
interest if it i.s reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiIl
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect
on the public generally, on the official 
or a member of his or her
imrnediate family or on:
(c) Any source of income aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or hore in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
officiaf within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is rnade.
Therefore, as a general ru1e, any person or business that has
made any payment to you in the past 12 months is a source of
incorne io-yo, for the purPoses of Section 87103(c) ' Thus, North
coast, u= |ort empl-oyei, would be a source of incorne to you, and
2 Part of this inforrnation $/as set forth in
of this .infornation was obtained from you in
telephone conversation of February 22t L99].'
your letter and Part
the course of our


Page 3
would continue to be a source of income fo豊
互土量圭量量置士畳テ百ユニ上皇ェ_上h全
last pavment North Coast made to vou of S2
However, Regulation 18704 (copy enclosed) provides a linited
exception which appears to apply to your situation. 
Pursuant to
Regulation 7a7o4, a former emptoyer is not a source of income
under section 871-03 (c) if all of the following apply: (1) A11
income from the employer was received by or accrued to the public
official 
prior to the tirne he or she became a public official,
and; (2) The income was received in the normal course of the
previous enployment; and, (3) There was no expectation by the
pubfic official- at the time he or she assuned office of renewed
Lmployment with the forner enployer. (See AIlen Advice Letter,
No. A-eS-r96,' Ruiz Advice Letter, No. A-84-268, copies enclosed.)
If Regufation 18704 appl-ies to your situation, North Coast will
not be considered to be a source of income to you which might lead
to a conflict of interest.{
If you have any further questions regarding this natter,
please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-590L-
S incere Iy,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral Counsel
hn W.
wa I Iace
Legal Division
■′
sH : JWw: aa
Enc Iosure s
3 Further, Section 82030 provides that the income of an
individual also includes a pro-rata share of any income of any
business entity or trust in which the individual or spouse owns,
directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 1o-percent interest or
greater. 
(RusselI Advice Letter, No. A-88-484, copy enclosed.)
frowever, you stated you have no ownership interest in North coagt.
Therefore, the clients of North Coast are not treated as economic
interests of yours.
4 If you deternine that Regulation 18704 does not apply and that
North coast is a "source of incomerr to you under the Act, you nay
want to contact us for further advice as to $/hether this would
required you to disqualify yourself from rnaking or participating
in a particular decision.

QUESTION: As Director of Public works and city Engineer for the city of
El Paso de Robles may you participate in governmental decisions
which rnay affect your former enployer?

CONCLUSION: According to your facts, it does not appear that your prior
employment with uorth Coast Engineering, Inc. will result in a
conflict of interest under the PoIitica1 Reform Act.
